checks.notes: All TestCatalog* tests pass, including TestCatalogConcurrentDistinctFileIDs (previously failed with a real torn-read/lost-update bug after removing fmMu, fixed by adding pageStripes) and TestCatalogFileManagerNarrowLockDoesNotSerializeAcrossIO (new contention proof).

1.1.3 FileManager tests (TestCatalogFileManager) still pass unchanged with the new internal mu locking; TestCatalogFileManagerNarrowLockDoesNotSerializeAcrossIO also matches this -run pattern and passes.

Full engine/catalog suite green under -race: Catalog, FileManager, FileIDAllocator, Page, Record tests all pass. ~63-67s wall time (dominated by pre-existing TestFileIDAllocator concurrent test, unrelated to this fix).

matrix_coverage.narrow_lock_guards_only_shared_state: covered by file_test.go's existing TestCatalogFileManager suite (unchanged pass) plus manual code review of file.go's mu field/comment
matrix_coverage.fmMu_removed_from_catalog: confirmed by grep: no remaining references to fmMu in catalog.go
matrix_coverage.concurrent_ops_different_fileIDs_do_not_: covered by TestCatalogStripesDoNotSerializeAcrossDifferentFileIDs (record-stripe level, unchanged) and the new TestCatalogFileManagerNarrowLockDoesNotSerializeAcrossIO (FileManager-internal-lock level)
matrix_coverage.same_physical_page_correctness_preserved: covered by TestCatalogConcurrentDistinctFileIDs (300 fileIDs x 4 workers, round-trip + final-state assertions, -race clean) — this is what caught the pageStripes gap during development of this fix
matrix_coverage.doc_comment_accuracy: manually reviewed catalog.go's Catalog doc comment; now describes stripes + indexMu + pageStripes + FileManager's own internal lock accurately
